turnaround_reason: Consumer paperboard packaging name down 54% from $24 highs to $10.82 on the consumer-staples destocking cycle plus tariff/cost inflation hitting margins. Q1 2026 operating margin collapsed to 0.9% from 10.4% prior year. Management responding with $60M annualized cost cuts via workforce reduction, network optimization, and contractual price recovery mechanisms. Q1 EPS beat by 48% and stock had +8% pre-market reaction.
bullish_points: Q1 2026 EPS beat consensus by 48% ($0.09 vs $0.06) despite the margin compression — cost actions are working
Adjusted EBITDA margin held at 10.8% on $232M EBITDA — underlying franchise still works
Volume turned positive (+1% YoY), improving as Q1 progressed — destocking cycle appears to be ending
$60M cost reduction program clearly defined: workforce, network, capex discipline
Trading at 0.99x EV/Revenue, 7x EV/EBITDA — typical industrial-cyclical bottom valuation
bearish_points: Debt $5.8B vs $190M cash; debt-to-equity 178% — leverage limits margin-recovery beta to equity
Operating margin compression (10.4 → 0.9%) is severe; recovery to even half of prior margins requires real pricing action
Tariffs on steel/aluminum/imports are an unmitigated headwind — not all under management control
Free cash flow $187M — barely covers $300M+ in annual interest expense
9 analysts, 'Hold' rating, target mean $11.79 = effectively at current price; no sell-side conviction
catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings July 28 — first full quarter under the $60M cost program; commentary on bleached cupstock pricing and tariff pass-through
suggested_trade: Small spot position or Jan 2027 $12 calls. The recovery is real but slow; the leverage means equity multiple expansion is capped until debt comes down. This is a cycle play, not a story stock — keep position modest.
key_risk: Tariffs persist or escalate; CPG customers continue destocking through 2026; debt refinancing in 2027-2028 at higher rates.
would_skip_if: Q2 operating margin doesn't expand sequentially; volumes turn negative again; CPG customers like Procter & Gamble guide weaker for 2H
